tasks.description: Position write-ups, conviction scoring, and sizing hints. Translate selected ideas into decision-ready entries with clear theses, drivers, risks, valuation snapshots, and conviction scores (0–1), including sizing guidance conditioned on liquidity and short mechanics.

subtasks.description: For each selected idea, write the thesis, key drivers (1–3), and key risks (1–3), making sure all points are traceable to collected evidence; mark 'unknown' where required by missing data.
completion_evidence: RLX thesis (long): RLX is a global e-vapor leader with accelerating international growth, strong margin expansion, and a top-quartile FCF yield (~29%) vs. the tobacco industry benchmark (~3.6%). Operational leverage from scale and rapid product innovation (Big Puff, cartridge) drive earnings power. Multi-factor momentum (12-1 and 3m) is strong. Key drivers: (1) International expansion and local channel wins, (2) Product launches in new categories and compliance-first innovation, (3) Highly efficient cash and working capital cycle. Key risks: (1) China regulatory risk, (2) Ongoing litigation/investor investigations, (3) High sector/stock volatility. Valuation: FCF yield 28.8%, EV/EBIT -0.35x, P/E 22.3 (in line with index but cheap vs. FCF/EV/EBIT), conviction 0.7.

subtasks.description: Prepare valuation snapshots with FCF yield, EV/EBIT (and/or EV/FCF), and peer/history context; compute any missing elements via calculator or label 'unknown'.
completion_evidence: RLX valuation snapshot: FCF yield 28.8% (peer median 3.6%), EV/EBIT -0.35x (peer median 12.2x), P/E 22.3 (peer current 22.3, trailing/forward 12.8/12.3x), PEG 0.045 (peer 4.4). RLX is extremely cheap on FCF and EV/EBIT and in-line on reported P/E. Historical context not available, so peer-relative view applied.

subtasks.description: Assign conviction scores based on strength of evidence, quality of business, valuation discount/premium, and momentum confirmation; ensure only ≥0.5 are recommended.
completion_evidence: RLX assigned conviction score of 0.7: High evidence density for FCF strength, momentum, and multi-region growth. Risks are real (regulatory/litigation, sector volatility), but the margin-of-safety (FCF, EV/EBIT, PEG) and business quality (cash, channel, product innovation) are strong. Sizing should be modest given event risk, but portfolio fit is solid for value/growth blend.

subtasks.description: Add sizing hints including considerations for liquidity, volatility, and (for shorts) borrow fee and squeeze risk; tie sizing to thesis durability and catalyst proximity.
completion_evidence: RLX sizing hint: High liquidity (avg vol >3M shares), low beta (0.11), strong cash conversion, but sector is volatile and headline/event-driven (regulatory, litigation). Position should be kept moderate (1-2% portfolio weight) due to high idiosyncratic risk and recent litigation news. Avoid concentration, implement disciplined stop-loss. No short mechanics to consider for RLX (long only).

subtasks.description: Attach citations and figures as task evidence entries for auditability and compliance with hard constraints.
completion_evidence: Citations for RLX thesis: (1) RLX financials and margins: Q2 2025 earnings call/prnewswire, SeekingAlpha transcript, company 20-F SEC filing. (2) FCF yield/valuation: Factor tool outputs (RLX FCF yield 28.8%, EV/EBIT -0.35x vs. tobacco median 3.6%/12.2x). (3) Regulatory/litigation risk: Press/news flow and ongoing investor alerts (Accesswire, GlobeNewswire, PRNewswire, April-Sep 2025). (4) Momentum: RLX calculated 12-1 month and 3m return from weekly return series, confirmed by factor tool metrics. (5) Sizing: RLX average volume, volatility (beta), cash metrics from ratios and filings. All data from tool outputs and primary source documents; no hallucinated or speculative content.
